title: Mï؟½Andrewï؟½s Hymn
content:
آ آ آ آ Lord, Thou hast made this world below the shadow of a dream,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½, taught by time, I takï؟½ it so, exceptinï؟½ always Steam.
آ آ آ آ From coupler-flange to spindle-guide I see Thy Hand, O God,
آ آ آ آ Predestination in the stride oï؟½ yon connectinï؟½-rod.
آ آ آ آ John Calvin might haï؟½ forged the same, enorrmous, certain, slow,
آ آ آ آ Ay, wrought it in the furnace-flame, my ï؟½Institutioï؟½.
آ آ آ آ I cannot get my sleep to-night; old bones are hard to please;
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ll stand the middle watch up here, alone wiï؟½ God anï؟½ these
آ آ آ آ My engines, after ninety days oï؟½ race anï؟½ rack anï؟½ strain
آ آ آ آ Through all the seas of all Thy world, slam-banginï؟½ home again.
آ آ آ آ Slam-bang too much, they knock a wee, the crosshead-gibs are loose;
آ آ آ آ But thirty thousand mile oï؟½ sea has gied them fair excuse. . . .
آ آ آ آ Fine, clear anï؟½ dark, a full-draught breeze, wiï؟½ Ushant out oï؟½ sight,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ Ferguson relievinï؟½ Hay. Old girl, yeï؟½ll walk to-night!
آ آ آ آ His wifeï؟½s at Plymouth. . . . Seventy, One, Two, Three since he began,
آ آ آ آ Three turns for Mistress Ferguson. . .and whoï؟½s to blame the man?
آ آ آ آ Thereï؟½s none at any port for me, by drivinï؟½ fast or slow,
آ آ آ آ Since Elsie Campbell went to Thee, Lord, thirty years ago.
آ آ آ آ (The year the Sarah Sands was burned. Oh roads we used to tread,
آ آ آ آ Fraï؟½ Maryhill to Pollokshaws, fraï؟½ Govan to Parkhead!)
آ آ آ آ Not but theyï؟½re ceevil on the Board. Yeï؟½ll hear Sir Kenneth say:
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Good-morrn, Mï؟½Andrew! Back again? Anï؟½ howï؟½s your bilge to-day?ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Miscallinï؟½ technicalities but handinï؟½ me my chair
آ آ آ آ To drink Madeira wiï؟½ three Earls, the auld Fleet Engineer,
آ آ آ آ That started as a boiler-whelp, when steam and he were low.
آ آ آ آ I mind the time we used to serve a broken pipe wiï؟½ tow.
آ آ آ آ Ten pound was all the pressure then, Eh! Eh!, a man wad drive;
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ here, our workinï؟½ gauges give one hunder fifty-five!
آ آ آ آ Weï؟½re creepinï؟½ on wiï؟½ each new rig, less weight anï؟½ larger power:
آ آ آ آ Thereï؟½ll be the loco-boiler next anï؟½ thirty knots an hour!
آ آ آ آ Thirty anï؟½ more. What I haï؟½ seen since ocean-steam began
آ آ آ آ Leaves me no doot for the machine: but what about the man?
آ آ آ آ The man that counts, wiï؟½ all his runs, one million mile oï؟½ sea:
آ آ آ آ Four time the span from earth to moon. . . . How far, O Lord, from Thee?
آ آ آ آ That wast beside him night anï؟½ day. Ye mind my first typhoon?
آ آ آ آ It scoughed the skipper on his way to jock wiï؟½ the saloon.
آ آ آ آ Three feet were on the stokehold-floor, just slappinï؟½ to anï؟½ fro,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ cast me on a furnace-door. I have the marks to show.
آ آ آ آ Marks! I haï؟½ marks oï؟½ more than burns, deep in my soul anï؟½ black,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ times like this, when things go smooth, my wickudness comes back.
آ آ آ آ The sins oï؟½ four and forty years, all up anï؟½ down the seas,
آ آ آ آ Clack anï؟½ repeat like valves half-fed. . . . Forgieï؟½s our trespasses.
آ آ آ آ Nights when Iï؟½d come on deck to mark, wiï؟½ envy in my gaze,
آ آ آ آ The couples kittlinï؟½ in the dark between the funnel stays;
آ آ آ آ Years when I raked the ports wiï؟½ pride to fill my cup oï؟½ wrong,
آ آ آ آ Judge not, O Lord, my steps aside at Gay Street in Hong-Kong!
آ آ آ آ Blot out the wastrel hours of mine in sin when I abode,
آ آ آ آ Jane Harriganï؟½s anï؟½ Number Nine, The Reddick anï؟½ Grant Road!
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ waur than all, my crowninï؟½ sin, rank blasphemy anï؟½ wild.
آ آ آ آ I was not four and twenty then, Ye wadna judge a child?
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½d seen the Tropics first that run, new fruit, new smells, new air,
آ آ آ آ How could I tell, blind-fou wiï؟½ sun, the Deil was lurkinï؟½ there?
آ آ آ آ By day like playhouse-scenes the shore slid past our sleepy eyes;
آ آ آ آ By night those soft, lasceevious stars leered from those velvet skies,
آ آ آ آ In port (we used no cargo-steam) Iï؟½d daunder down the streets,
آ آ آ آ An ijjit grinninï؟½ in a dream, for shells anï؟½ parrakeets,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ walkinï؟½-sticks oï؟½ carved bamboo anï؟½ blowfish stuffed anï؟½ dried,
آ آ آ آ Fillinï؟½ my bunk wiï؟½ rubbishry the Chief put overside.
آ آ آ آ Till, off Sambawa Head, Ye mind, I heard a land-breeze caï؟½,
آ آ آ آ Milk-warm wiï؟½ breath oï؟½ spice anï؟½ bloom: ï؟½Mï؟½Andrew, come awaï؟½!ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Firm, clear anï؟½ low, no haste, no hate, the ghostly whisper went,
آ آ آ آ Just statinï؟½ eevidential facts beyonï؟½ all argument:
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Your mitherï؟½s Godï؟½s a graspinï؟½ deil, the shadow oï؟½ yourselï؟½,
آ آ آ آ Got out oï؟½ books by meenisters clean daft on Heaven anï؟½ Hell.
آ آ آ آ They makï؟½ Him in the Broomielaw, oï؟½ Glasgie cold anï؟½ dirt,
آ آ آ آ A jealous, pridefuï؟½ fetich, lad, thatï؟½s only strong to hurt,
آ آ آ آ Yeï؟½ll not go back to Him again anï؟½ kiss His red-hot rod,
آ آ آ آ But come wiï؟½ Usï؟½ (Now, who were They?) ï؟½anï؟½ know the Leevinï؟½ God,
آ آ آ آ That does not kipper souls for sport or break a life in jest,
آ آ آ آ But swells the ripeninï؟½ cocoanuts anï؟½ ripes the womanï؟½s breast.ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ there it stopped: cut off: no more; that quiet, certain voice,
آ آ آ آ For me, six months oï؟½ twenty-four, to leave or take at choice.
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Twas on me like a thunderclap, it racked me through anï؟½ through,
آ آ آ آ Temptation past the show oï؟½ speech, unnameable anï؟½ new,
آ آ آ آ The Sin against the Holy Ghost? . . . Anï؟½ under all, our screw.
آ آ آ آ That storm blew by but left behind her anchor-shiftinï؟½ swell,
آ آ آ آ Thou knowest all my heart anï؟½ mind, Thou knowest, Lord, I fell.
آ آ آ آ Third on the Mary Gloster then, and first that night in Hell!
آ آ آ آ Yet was Thy hand beneath my head, about my feet Thy care,
آ آ آ آ Fraï؟½ Deli clear to Torres Strait, the trial oï؟½ despair,
آ آ آ آ But when we touched the Barrier Reef Thy answer to my prayer!
آ آ آ آ We dared not run that sea by night but lay anï؟½ held our fire,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ I was drowsinï؟½ on the hatch, sick, sick wiï؟½ doubt anï؟½ tire:
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Better the sight of eyes that see than wanderinï؟½ oï؟½ desire!ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Ye mind that word? Clear as our gongs, again, anï؟½ once again,
آ آ آ آ When rippinï؟½ down through coral-trash ran out our moorinï؟½-chain;
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ by Thy Grace I had the Light to see my duty plain.
آ آ آ آ Light on the engine-room, no more, bright as our carbons burn.
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ve lost it since a thousand times, but never past return.
آ آ آ آ .آ آ آ آ  .آ آ آ آ  .آ آ آ آ  .آ آ آ آ  .
آ آ آ آ Obsairve. Per annum weï؟½ll have here two thousand souls aboard,
آ آ آ آ Think not I dare to justify myself before the Lord,
آ آ آ آ But, average fifteen hunder souls safe-borne fraï؟½ port to port,
آ آ آ آ I am oï؟½ service to my kind. Ye wadna blame the thought?
آ آ آ آ Maybe they steam from grace to wrath, to sin by folly led,,
آ آ آ آ It isna mine to judge their path, their lives are on my head.
آ آ آ آ Mine at the last, when all is done it all comes back to me,
آ آ آ آ The fault that leaves six thousand ton a log upon the sea.
آ آ آ آ Weï؟½ll takï؟½ one stretch, three weeks anï؟½ odd by any road ye steer,
آ آ آ آ Fraï؟½ Cape Town east to Wellington, ye need an engineer.
آ آ آ آ Fail there, yeï؟½ve time to weld your shaft, ay, eat it, ere yeï؟½re spoke;
آ آ آ آ Or make Kerguelen under sail, three jiggers burned wiï؟½ smoke!
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ home again, the Rio run: itï؟½s no childï؟½s play to go
آ آ آ آ Steaminï؟½ to bell for fourteen days oï؟½ snow anï؟½ floe anï؟½ blow,
آ آ آ آ The bergs like kelpies overside that girn anï؟½ turn anï؟½ shift
آ آ آ آ Whaur, grindinï؟½ like the Mills oï؟½ God, goes by the big South drift.
آ آ آ آ (Hail, snow anï؟½ ice that praise the Lord: Iï؟½ve met them at their work,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ wished we had anither route or they anither kirk.)
آ آ آ آ Yonï؟½s strain, hard strain, oï؟½ head anï؟½ hand, for though Thy Power brings
آ آ آ آ All skill to naught, Yeï؟½ll understand a man must think oï؟½ things.
آ آ آ آ Then, at the last, weï؟½ll get to port anï؟½ hoist their baggage clear,
آ آ آ آ The passengers, wiï؟½ gloves anï؟½ canes, anï؟½ this is what Iï؟½ll hear:
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Well, thank ye for a pleasant voyage. The tenderï؟½s cominï؟½ now.ï؟½
آ آ آ آ While I go testinï؟½ follower-bolts anï؟½ watch the skipper bow.
آ آ آ آ Theyï؟½ve words for every one but me, shake hands wiï؟½ half the crew,
آ آ آ آ Except the dour Scots engineer, the man they never knew.
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ yet I like the wark for all weï؟½ve damï؟½ few pickinï؟½s here,
آ آ آ آ No pension, anï؟½ the most we earnï؟½s four hunder pound a year.
آ آ آ آ Better myself abroad? Maybe. Iï؟½d sooner starve than sail
آ آ آ آ Wiï؟½ such as call a snifter-rod ross. . .French for nightingale.
آ آ آ آ Commeesion on my stores? Some do; but I can not afford
آ آ آ آ To lie like stewards wiï؟½ patty-pans, . Iï؟½m older than the Board.
آ آ آ آ A bonus on the coal I save? Ou ay, the Scots are close,
آ آ آ آ But when I grudge the strength Ye gave Iï؟½ll grudge their food to those.
آ آ آ آ (Thereï؟½s bricks that I might recommend, anï؟½ clink the fire-bars cruel.
آ آ آ آ No! Welsh, Wangarti at the worst, anï؟½ damn all patent fuel!)
آ آ آ آ Inventions? Ye must stay in port to makï؟½ a patent pay.
آ آ آ آ My Deeferential Valve-Gear taught me how that business lay,
آ آ آ آ I blame no chaps wiï؟½ clearer head for aught they make or sell.
آ آ آ آ I found that I could not invent anï؟½ look to these, as well.
آ آ آ آ So, wrestled wiï؟½ Apollyon, Nah!, fretted like a bairn,
آ آ آ آ But burned the workinï؟½-plans last run wiï؟½ all I hoped to earn.
آ آ آ آ Ye know how hard an Idol dies, anï؟½ what that meant to me,
آ آ آ آ Eï؟½en takï؟½ it for a sacrifice acceptable to Thee. . . .
آ آ آ آ Below there! Oiler! Whatï؟½s your wark? Ye find it runninï؟½ hard?
آ آ آ آ Ye neednï؟½t swill the cap wiï؟½ oil, this isnï؟½t the Cunard!
آ آ آ آ Ye thought? Ye are not paid to think. Go, sweat that off again!
آ آ آ آ Tck! Tck! Itï؟½s deeficult to sweer nor takï؟½ The Name in vain!
آ آ آ آ Men, ay anï؟½ women, call me stern. Wiï؟½ these to oversee
آ آ آ آ Yeï؟½ll note Iï؟½ve little time to burn on social repartee.
آ آ آ آ The bairns see what their elders miss; theyï؟½ll hunt me to anï؟½ fro,
آ آ آ آ Till for the sake of, well, a kiss, I takï؟½ ï؟½em down below.
آ آ آ آ That minds me of our Viscount loon, Sir Kennethï؟½s kin, the chap
آ آ آ آ Wiï؟½ Russia leather tennis-shoon anï؟½ spar-decked yachtinï؟½-cap.
آ آ آ آ I showed him round last week, oï؟½er all, anï؟½ at the last says he:
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Mister Mï؟½Andrew, donï؟½t you think steam spoils romance at sea?ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Damned ijjit! Iï؟½d been doon that morn to see what ailed the throws,
آ آ آ آ Manholinï؟½, on my back, the cranks three inches off my nose.
آ آ آ آ Romance! Those first-class passengers they like it very well,
آ آ آ آ Printed anï؟½ bound in little books; but why donï؟½t poets tell?
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½m sick of all their quirks anï؟½ turns, the loves anï؟½ doves they dream,
آ آ آ آ Lord, send a man like Robbie Burns to sing the Song oï؟½ Steam!
آ آ آ آ To match wiï؟½ Scotiaï؟½s noblest speech yon orchestra sublime
آ آ آ آ Whaurto, uplifted like the Just, the tail-rods mark the time.
آ آ آ آ The crank-throws give the double-bass, the feed-pump sobs anï؟½ heaves,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ now the main eccentrics start their quarrel on the sheaves:
آ آ آ آ Her time, her own appointed time, the rocking link-head bides,
آ آ آ آ Till, hear that note?, the rodï؟½s return whings glimmerinï؟½ through the guides.
آ آ آ آ Theyï؟½re all awaï؟½! True beat, full power, the clanginï؟½ chorus goes
آ آ آ آ Clear to the tunnel where they sit, my purrinï؟½ dynamos.
آ آ آ آ Interdependence absolute, foreseen, ordained, decreed,
آ آ آ آ To work, Yeï؟½ll note, at any tilt anï؟½ every rate oï؟½ speed.
آ آ آ آ Fraï؟½ skylight-lift to furnace-bars, backed, bolted, braced anï؟½ stayed,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ singinï؟½ like the Morninï؟½ Stars for joy that they are made;
آ آ آ آ While, out oï؟½ touch oï؟½ vanity, the sweatinï؟½ thrust-block says:
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Not unto us the praise, or man, not unto us the praise!ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Now, aï؟½ together, hear them lift their lesson, theirs anï؟½ mine:
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Law, Orrder, Duty anï؟½ Restraint, Obedience, Discipline!ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Mill, forge anï؟½ try-pit taught them that when roarinï؟½ they arose,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ whiles I wonder if a soul was gied them wiï؟½ the blows.
آ آ آ آ Oh for a man to weld it then, in one trip-hammer strain,
آ آ آ آ Till even first-class passengers could tell the meaninï؟½ plain!
آ آ آ آ But no one cares except myselï؟½ that serve anï؟½ understand
آ آ آ آ My seven thousand horse-power here. Eh, Lord! Theyï؟½re grand, theyï؟½re grand!
آ آ آ آ Uplift am I? When first in store the new-made beasties stood,
آ آ آ آ Were Ye cast down that breathed the Word declarinï؟½ all things good?
آ آ آ آ Not so! Oï؟½ that warld-liftinï؟½ joy no after-fall could vex,
آ آ آ آ Yeï؟½ve left a glimmer still to cheer the Man, the Arrtifex!
آ آ آ آ That holds, in spite oï؟½ knock and scale, oï؟½ friction, waste anï؟½ slip,
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ by that light, now, mark my word, weï؟½ll build the Perfect Ship.
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ll never last to judge her lines or take her curve, not I.
آ آ آ آ But I haï؟½ lived anï؟½ I haï؟½ worked. ï؟½Be thanks to Thee, Most High!
آ آ آ آ Anï؟½ I haï؟½ done what I haï؟½ done, judge Thou if ill or well,
آ آ آ آ Always Thy Grace preventinï؟½ me. . . .
آ آ آ آ Losh! Yonï؟½s the ï؟½Stand byï؟½ bell.
آ آ آ آ Pilot so soon? His flare it is. The morninï؟½-watch is set.
آ آ آ آ Well, God be thanked, as I was sayinï؟½, Iï؟½m no Pelagian yet.
آ آ آ آ Now Iï؟½ll takï؟½ on. . . .

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Morrn, Ferguson. Man, have ye ever thought
آ آ آ آ What your good leddy costs in coal? . . . Iï؟½ll burn ï؟½em down to port.
meaning: About: Joseph Rudyard Kipling (December 30, 1865 ï؟½ January 18, 1936) was an English author and poet, born in Bombay, India, and best known for his works The Jungle Book (1894), The Second Jungle Book (1895), Just So Stories (1902), and Puck of Pook's Hill (1906); his novel, Kim (1901); his poems, including Mandalay (1890), Gunga Din (1890), and "Ifï؟½" (1910); and his many short stories, including "The Man Who Would Be King" (1888) and the collections Life's Handicap (1891), The Day's Work (1898), and Plain Tales from the Hills (1888). He is regarded as a major "innovator in the art of the short story"; his children's books are enduring classics of children's literature; and his best work speaks to a versatile and luminous narrative gift.
